svg.text: Accelerating Integer Factorization via Multi-Modulus Quadratic Residue Sieving
If a − b > 1 and a + b < N, both factors are nontrivial — Lean-verified ✓
2. Quadratic Residue Sieve — Eliminating Impossible Candidates
Key insight: a² − N must be a perfect square, so (a² − N) mod m must be a quadratic residue
Sieve Principle (Lean-verified): If (a² − N) mod m is NOT a QR, no b exists ⟹ skip a entirely
theorem residue_sieve_contrapositive: ¬(∃ c, c² % m = (a² − N) % m) → ¬(∃ b, N = a² − b²)
Each additional coprime modulus multiplies elimination power — approaching 20× speedup with 8 moduli
Precompute QR sets for moduli {m₁, m₂, ..., mₖ}

g.text: All proofs machine-verified in Lean 4 + Mathlib

svg.text: Harmonic Residue Factorization • Formally Verified in Lean 4 • Open Source
